edges.description: Loss of ORC1/ORNT1 function directly impairs mitochondrial ornithine transport.
Impaired mitochondrial ornithine entry causes systemic ornithine accumulation.
Rare ocular involvement is reported in the HHH phenotype spectrum and is linked here to the chronic hyperornithinemic transport-defect state.
Rare chorioretinal hypopigmentation is represented as ocular involvement downstream of the chronic ornithine transport defect.
Mitochondrial ornithine deficiency reduces ornithine-dependent urea-cycle flux.
Impaired urea-cycle nitrogen disposal produces elevated ammonia.
Elevated ammonia during impaired urea-cycle flux drives acute encephalopathic decompensation.
Reduced ornithine availability leaves carbamoyl phosphate available for diversion into orotic acid and homocitrulline pathways.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced ornithine availability limits normal ornithine transcarbamylase substrate flux.

edges.description: The urea-cycle transport defect is associated with acute and chronic hepatic dysfunction, transaminase elevation, and coagulation abnormalities.
intermediate_mechanisms: Metabolic decompensation with hepatic nitrogen-disposal stress.

edges.description: Hyperammonemic urea-cycle decompensation can present with tachypnea and respiratory alkalosis.
Chronic nitrogen intolerance can manifest as aversion to protein-rich foods.
intermediate_mechanisms: Protein intake increases nitrogen load when urea-cycle flux is impaired.

edges.description: Recurrent metabolic stress and chronic protein intolerance contribute to poor growth.
Disturbed mitochondrial ornithine entry perturbs urea-cycle intermediate handling, including circulating citrulline.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced ornithine availability alters ornithine transcarbamylase substrate flux and downstream citrulline production.

edges.description: Carbamoyl-phosphate diversion produces urinary homocitrulline.
Orotic acid abnormalities can occur as part of the diverted carbamoyl-phosphate state.
Hyperammonemia manifests clinically as acute encephalopathy.
Acute hyperammonemic encephalopathy can present with lethargy.
Hyperammonemic encephalopathy can manifest as episodic confusion.
Severe acute hyperammonemic encephalopathy can progress to coma.
Acute metabolic decompensation includes intermittent vomiting.
Neonatal or infantile hyperammonemic decompensation can present with poor feeding.
Acute metabolic decompensation can include tachypnea.
Hyperammonemic brain dysfunction can include seizures during the HHH disease course.
Myoclonic seizures occur within the chronic and episodic neurologic HHH phenotype.
Recurrent metabolic injury and disease-specific mechanisms are associated with chronic neurocognitive and pyramidal tract involvement.
intermediate_mechanisms: Progressive neurologic involvement described during the disease course.
edges.target: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Pyramidal tract involvement can manifest as spastic paraplegia.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Progressive upper-motor-neuron involvement produces abnormal pyramidal signs.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Upper-motor-neuron involvement commonly manifests as hyperreflexia.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Pyramidal tract dysfunction can manifest as clonus.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Pyramidal tract dysfunction can produce a spastic gait pattern.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Progressive neurologic involvement includes variable cognitive impairment.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurocognitive involvement includes developmental delay and intellectual disability.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurologic involvement often includes neurodevelopmental delay.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic cognitive involvement can manifest as learning disability.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurologic involvement includes progressive ataxia.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Cerebellar and motor-system involvement can manifest as poor coordination.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Early neurologic involvement can include generalized hypotonia.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurologic involvement can include sensory pathway impairment.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurodevelopmental and motor-planning involvement can manifest as speech apraxia.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurologic injury is associated with cerebral cortical atrophy in the HHH phenotype spectrum.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic neurologic disease can include multifocal cerebral white matter abnormalities.
edges.source: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

edges.description: Hepatic involvement manifests as decreased liver function.
HHH hepatic involvement can include hepatitis-like attacks.
Severe hepatic involvement can progress to hepatic failure in rare cases.
Hepatic involvement can manifest as hepatomegaly.
Liver injury in HHH syndrome is reflected by elevated circulating hepatic transaminases.
Elevated hepatic transaminases provide a biochemical readout of the liver-dysfunction branch.
Hepatic dysfunction can be accompanied by coagulation abnormalities.
Elevated ornithine reports failed mitochondrial ornithine uptake due to ORC1/ORNT1 transport deficiency.
Elevated ammonia reports impaired hepatic urea-cycle nitrogen disposal.
Urinary homocitrulline reports carbamoyl-phosphate diversion when ornithine-dependent urea-cycle flux is impaired.
Oroticaciduria reports diversion of excess carbamoyl phosphate into pyrimidine-pathway products.
Elevated hepatic transaminases report the liver-dysfunction branch of HHH syndrome.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011965 | Abnormality of citrulline metabolism | Very frequent (99-80%)
HP:0003256 | Abnormality of the coagulation cascade | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: The major acute clinical presentation found was encephalopathy and liver dysfunction.

nodes.description: Acute metabolic decompensation requires urgent correction of ammonia and metabolite imbalances to reduce risk of irreversible neurologic injury.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Mutation of ORNT1 Detected in a Hyperornithinemia-Hyperammonemia-Homocitrullinuria Syndrome Child by Clinical Whole-Exome Sequencing.
evidence.snippet: Correction of the accumulating metabolites is necessary to prevent irreversible neurological impairment.

nodes.description: Ammonia scavengers are used to control ammonia in HHH syndrome, providing alternative nitrogen-disposal routes when urea-cycle flux is impaired.

evidence.snippet: Acute and chronic management consists in the control of hyperammonemia with protein-restricted diet supplemented with citrulline/arginine and ammonia scavengers.

evidence.reference_title: Management of late onset urea cycle disorders-a remaining challenge for the intensivist?
evidence.snippet: Ammonia is diverted to the glycine and hippuric acid pathway by benzoate, and to the glutamine and phenylacetylglutamine pathway allowing elimination in the urine without passing through the urea cycle

nodes.description: When ornithine-dependent mitochondrial flux is impaired, excess carbamoyl phosphate is reflected clinically by urinary homocitrulline and often orotic acid abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: The diagnosis relies on clinical signs and the peculiar metabolic triad of hyperammonemia, hyperornithinemia, and urinary excretion of homocitrulline.
Three out of four cases presented with hyperammonemia, elevated transaminases, and uraciluria.

nodes.description: Chronic HHH syndrome commonly involves neurodevelopmental delay, cognitive impairment, learning disability, ataxia, spastic paraplegia, hyperreflexia, clonus, and other pyramidal signs.
nodes.id: Chronic Neurocognitive and Pyramidal Tract Dysfunction

evidence.snippet: Alternatively, patients show a chronic course with aversion for protein rich foods, developmental delay/intellectual disability, myoclonic seizures, ataxia and pyramidal dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: Nervous system involvement was common, progressive, and presented with signs of upper motor neuron disease as well as variable degrees of cognitive impairment.

nodes.description: Citrulline or arginine supplementation is used with dietary management to support urea-cycle intermediate availability and biochemical control.

Three out of four cases presented with hyperammonemia, elevated transaminases, and uraciluria.

nodes.description: Hyperammonemic crises produce acute encephalopathy with vomiting, confusion, lethargy, and coma; recurrent or severe episodes contribute to long-term neurologic injury.

evidence.snippet: Acute clinical signs include intermittent episodes of vomiting, confusion or coma and hepatitis-like attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: The major acute clinical presentation found was encephalopathy and liver dysfunction.
All four cases demonstrated hyperornithinemia and prolonged prothrombin time.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated ornithine reports failed mitochondrial ornithine uptake due to ORC1/ORNT1 transport deficiency.

nodes.description: HHH syndrome frequently includes decreased liver function, transaminase elevation, hepatitis-like attacks, and coagulation abnormalities; severe cases can present with hepatic failure.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: The major acute clinical presentation found was encephalopathy and liver dysfunction.

nodes.description: Defective ORC1 transport prevents normal movement of ornithine into mitochondria, disrupting the mitochondrial entry point needed for ornithine-dependent urea-cycle flux.

evidence.snippet: HHH syndrome is caused by impaired ornithine transport across the inner mitochondrial membrane due to mutations in SLC25A15 gene, which encodes for the mitochondrial ornithine carrier ORC1.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: HHH is caused by biallelic disease-causing variants in the SLC25A15 gene.

readouts.interpretation: Oroticaciduria reports diversion of excess carbamoyl phosphate into pyrimidine-pathway products.

nodes.description: Chronic dietary protein restriction lowers nitrogen load and is a core long-term strategy for controlling hyperammonemia.

evidence.snippet: Acute and chronic management consists in the control of hyperammonemia with protein-restricted diet supplemented with citrulline/arginine and ammonia scavengers.

nodes.description: Biallelic SLC25A15 pathogenic variants reduce ORC1/ORNT1 mitochondrial ornithine carrier function, the initiating molecular lesion in HHH syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Mutation of ORNT1 Detected in a Hyperornithinemia-Hyperammonemia-Homocitrullinuria Syndrome Child by Clinical Whole-Exome Sequencing.
evidence.snippet: Hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria (HHH) syndrome, an inborn error of metabolism, is an inherited syndrome caused by loss-of-function mutations in the SLC25A15, resulting in ornithine translocase1 (ORNT1) deficiency.

nodes.description: Recurrent homozygous frameshift variant reported in Palestinian patients with HHH syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: The remaining four patients had a recurrent homozygous frameshift variant; NM_014252.4:c.446delG, (p.Ser149ThrfsTer45).

nodes.description: Novel homozygous exon 5 frameshift deletion reported in nine Palestinian patients with HHH syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical heterogeneity of hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria syndrome in thirteen palestinian patients and report of a novel variant in the SLC25A15 gene.
evidence.snippet: Analysis of the SLC25A15 gene sequence revealed a novel homozygous frameshift deletion in exon 5, NM_014252.4:c.552-555delTTTC; p (Phe185SerfsTer8) in nine patients.

nodes.description: Compound heterozygous SLC25A15 variants predicted to cause ORNT1 loss of function in a child diagnosed by whole-exome sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Mutation of ORNT1 Detected in a Hyperornithinemia-Hyperammonemia-Homocitrullinuria Syndrome Child by Clinical Whole-Exome Sequencing.
evidence.snippet: After biochemical confirmation of HHH, whole-exome sequencing (WES) was performed, which identified mutations at codons 21 and 179 of SLC25A15 that are predicted to result in the loss of function of ORNT1.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia Is a Common Phenotypic Finding in ARG1 Deficiency, P5CS Deficiency and HHH Syndrome: Three Inborn Errors of Metabolism Caused by Alteration of an Interconnected Pathway of Glutamate and Urea Cycle Metabolism.
evidence.snippet: Hyperornithinemia-Hyperammonemia-Homocitrullinuria syndrome, are clinically characterized by highly penetrant spastic paraplegia.

nodes.description: Reduced mitochondrial ornithine availability functionally impairs the urea cycle, lowering hepatic nitrogen disposal and producing hyperammonemia.

evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Hyperornithinemia-hyperammonemia-homocitrullinuria (HHH) syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder of the urea cycle.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia Is a Common Phenotypic Finding in ARG1 Deficiency, P5CS Deficiency and HHH Syndrome: Three Inborn Errors of Metabolism Caused by Alteration of an Interconnected Pathway of Glutamate and Urea Cycle Metabolism.
evidence.snippet: Interestingly, two urea cycle disorders, Argininemia and Hyperornithinemia-Hyperammonemia-Homocitrullinuria syndrome, are clinically characterized by highly penetrant spastic paraplegia.
